architecture.summary: A dual-path hybrid architecture combining EfficientNetV2 (hierarchical local features) and Vision Transformer (global contextual dependencies) with feature fusion. Auxiliary segmentation supervision via CheXmask anchors learned representations to lung and cardiac anatomy. Grad-CAM visual explanations are confined to anatomically valid regions. A neuro-symbolic reasoning layer applies fuzzy logic and radiological ontology to translate anatomically aligned neural activations into structured, human-readable diagnostic statements.
trained_components: neuro-symbolic reasoning layer (fuzzy logic + ontology mapping)

biblio.title: Anatomy-Guided Hybrid CNN–ViT Model with Neuro-Symbolic Reasoning for Early Diagnosis of Thoracic Diseases Multilabel

claims.statement: achieved a macro-AUROC of 0.9056 and a macro-accuracy of 93.9% across 14 pathologies
outstanding performance on emphysema (0.9694), hernia (0.9711), and cardiomegaly (0.9589)
external validation on the CheXpert dataset, yielding a macro-AUROC of 0.85
auxiliary segmentation supervision using CheXmask anchors the learned features to lung and cardiac anatomy, reducing reliance on spurious artifacts
Grad-CAM visual explanations are confined to clinically valid regions through anatomical basis
neuro-symbolic reasoning layer translates anatomically aligned neural activations into structured, human-readable diagnostic statements that explicitly articulate the model's clinical rationale
